p.em: Demonstrating O(log_B N) memory efficiency via Cache-Oblivious Layout

g.text: Logical Structure: Van Emde Boas Layout
Performance Metrics: vEB vs. Pointer BST

div.p: By grouping nodes into subtrees that fit exactly into cache lines or memory pages:

ul.li: Reduced DRAM Latency: By packing $\sqrt{N}$ subtrees contiguously, we reduce the number of times the CPU waits for data from main memory.
Cache Line Utilization: Standard BSTs waste ~60 bytes of a 64-byte fetch. vEB uses nearly 100% of the fetched cache line for path traversal.
Complexity: Total Memory Transfers $W \approx \log_B N$, significantly outperforming $O(\log_2 N)$.

div.p: BFS Index: 0 vEB Offset: 0 Compare: 750,000 > 524,287 Action: RIGHT
BFS Index: 2 vEB Offset: 2 Compare: 750,000 < 786,431 Action: LEFT
BFS Index: 5 vEB Offset: 5 Compare: 750,000 > 655,359 Action: RIGHT
Subtree ID: 750 vEB Base: 1,023 + (750 * 1,023) Pointer: &keys[768273] Action: Reload Cache Line
